other_observers: Dan Maynard, Mark Peterson, Cole Wild, Bryan Patrick, Ric Olson; Steve Larson found the bird first.
specific_location: 12 miles north of Wild Horse on a small private ranch
description: The was an all-brown-backed small bird with a long pointed warbler bill. The crown was a warm brown. It had a light supercillium and a dark eyestripe. The undersides were light, some parts buff and other offwhite. The dorsal surface of the tail was rusty brown. The tail at times (when rump tilted up) looked short, but at other times it appeared to be long. The tibio-tarsus was bright pink, darkest pink at the joint. Without wingbars.Behaviors: This was a hungry bird that stayed in a 10 foot radius and ate "stuff" out of the mud, often opening its mouth incredibly wide as accentuated by its very long bill. The highest the bird went was about 8-14 inches off the ground (wet mud with very shallow water adjacent to its feeding spot).Call: non heardPlumage: alternate
photographer: Photographer: Bill Maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
